description_long: This object is a short sword or dagger featuring a straight, double-edged blade with a mottled, metallic texture, suggesting forged or patterned steel. The blade is relatively narrow and tapers to a sharp point, suitable for thrusting or stabbing. The crossguard is broad and flat, colored in a bright gold or brass tone, providing hand protection and a visual contrast to the blade. The handle is cylindrical, matching the crossguard in color and material, and appears smooth without visible grip texture or wrapping. There are no visible decorative engravings or embellishments on the blade, guard, or handle. The overall design is functional and simple, with a focus on utility rather than ornamentation.
description: A short, double-edged sword or dagger with a patterned steel blade and a gold-colored crossguard and handle, featuring a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade and the wide gold crossguard and handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and the rectangular cross-section of the handle and guard.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slimness of the blade and the handle's rectangular profile.
